creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->open

I checked, and believe this is actually a problem with upstream R or out 
math/R port.  According to the documentation fo GCC 3.4.2 and also GCC 4.3: 

`-shared' 
Produce a shared object which can then be linked with other 
objects to form an executable.  Not all systems support this 
option.  For predictable results, you must also specify the same 
set of options that were used to generate code (`-fpic', `-fPIC', 
or model suboptions) when you specify this option.(1) 

and 

(1) On some systems, `gcc -shared' needs to build supplementary stub 
code for constructors to work.  On multi-libbed systems, `gcc -shared' 
must select the correct support libraries to link against.  Failing to 
supply the correct flags may lead to subtle defects.  Supplying them in 
cases where they are not necessary is innocuous. 

Looking at the log provided in this PR, the individual object files are 
built with -fpic, but the link (which then fails) is not receiving this 
option. 

The error message we received actually also points in that direction, as 
pointed out by the original submitter. 

Rong-En, is this something you could help with?  I am afraid I have no 
idea about this port...
